chapter_context.summary: Samuel is sent to Bethlehem to anoint young David; Saul is troubled by an evil spirit and employs David to play the harp to soothe him.

outline.text: Samuel Sent to Bethlehem to Sacrifice and Find the New King
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Samuel Sent to Bethlehem to Sacrifice and Find the New King (16:1-5), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Eliab and Others Rejected; David Chosen and Anointed
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Eliab and Others Rejected; David Chosen and Anointed (16:6-13), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Saul Troubled by an Evil Spirit; David Hired to Play the Harp
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Saul Troubled by an Evil Spirit; David Hired to Play the Harp (16:14-23), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 16, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
